content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 23 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 111r–112v (LC PDF pp. 220–221) | **Source Images:** DEU_023_page_001.jpg, DEU_023_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 157 (pa_0157)

---

**1** No one whose testicles are crushed or whose male organ is cut off shall enter the assembly of the LORD.

**2** No one born of a forbidden union shall enter the assembly of the LORD. Even to the tenth generation, none of his descendants shall enter the assembly of the LORD.

**3** No Ammonite or Moabite shall enter the assembly of the LORD. Even to the tenth generation, none of them shall ever enter the assembly of the LORD, **4** because they did not meet you with bread and water on the way, when you came out of Egypt, and because they hired against you Balaam the son of Beor from Pethor of Mesopotamia to curse you. **5** But the LORD your God would not listen to Balaam; instead the LORD your God turned the curse into a blessing for you, because the LORD your God loved you. **6** You shall not seek their peace or their prosperity all your days forever.

**7** You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother. You shall not abhor an Egyptian, because you were a sojourner in his land. **8** The children born to them in the third generation may enter the assembly of the LORD.

**9** When you are encamped against your enemies, you shall keep yourself from every evil thing.

**10** If any man among you becomes unclean because of a nocturnal emission, he shall go outside the camp and shall not come inside the camp. **11** But when evening comes, he shall bathe himself in water, and when the sun sets he shall come inside the camp.

**12** You shall have a place outside the camp, and you shall go out to it. **13** And you shall have a peg with your tools; and when you sit down outside, you shall dig a hole with it and turn back and cover your excrement. **14** For the LORD your God walks in the midst of your camp, to deliver you and to give your enemies before you; therefore your camp must be holy, so that he may not see anything indecent among you and turn away from you.

**15** You shall not give up to his master a slave who has escaped from his master to you. **16** He shall dwell with you, in your midst, in the place that he shall choose within one of your towns, wherever it suits him. You shall not oppress him.

**17** There shall be no cult prostitute among the daughters of Israel, and there shall be no cult prostitute among the sons of Israel. **18** You shall not bring the fee of a prostitute or the wages of a dog into the house of the LORD your God in payment for any vow, for both of these are an abomination to the LORD your God.

**19** You shall not charge interest on loans to your brother—interest on money, interest on food, interest on anything that is lent for interest. **20** You may charge a foreigner interest, but you may not charge your brother interest, that the LORD your God may bless you in all that you undertake in the land that you are entering to take possession of it.

**21** If you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay fulfilling it, for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and you would be guilty of sin. **22** But if you refrain from vowing, you will not be guilty of sin. **23** You shall be careful to do what has passed your lips, for you voluntarily vowed to the LORD your God what you have spoken with your mouth.

**24** If you go into your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes, as many as you wish, but you shall not put any in your bag. **25** If you go into your neighbor's standing grain, you may pluck the ears with your hand, but you shall not put a sickle to your neighbor's standing grain.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

**LOGOS — Formal Equivalence Analysis, Deuteronomy 23**

**Note on Versification:** This chapter's verse numbering follows the MT. Verse 1 of MT ch. 23 = 22:30 in English versifications that move the prohibition on father's wife to close ch. 22. LOGOS follows MT numbering, beginning with the exclusions from the qahal. The chapter as assigned has 26 verses per MT.

**v.1 "assembly of the LORD" (qehal YHWH):** The qahal is the gathered covenant community for worship and governance. LXX: ἐκκλησία κυρίου ("assembly/church of the Lord"), from which the Greek word for "church" derives. The exclusions here are ritual-legal in nature.

**v.1 "whose testicles are crushed or whose male organ is cut off" (petsua' daka o kherut shafkhah):** The Hebrew is anatomically explicit; LOGOS maintains that explicitness in formal equivalence. These physical conditions disqualified a man from participation in the sacred assembly. LXX: θλαδίας ("one whose testicles are crushed") and ἀποκεκομμένος ("one cut off"), matching MT.

**v.2 "born of a forbidden union" (mamzer):** The term mamzer is debated: it may mean the offspring of an illicit union (incest or adultery) rather than simply illegitimacy in the modern sense. LXX: ἐκ πόρνης ("from a prostitute"), which is narrower. "Born of a forbidden union" follows the broader scholarly consensus on mamzer's legal meaning.

**v.3 "Ammonite or Moabite" — "even to the tenth generation":** The tenth generation is effectively permanent exclusion (cf. the contrast with Edom/Egypt at vv.7-8, who are admitted in the third generation). Ammon and Moab's offense is doubly stated: inhospitality and hiring Balaam.

**v.4 "Pethor of Mesopotamia" (Petor asher al-ha-nahar eretz bney amo):** Lit. "Pethor which is by the river, the land of the sons of his people." Mesopotamia (Aram-Naharaim = Aram of the Two Rivers) is the traditional rendering for the general region. "Mesopotamia" is used as the conventional geographical gloss.

**v.5 "turned the curse into a blessing" (wayahafokh... ha-qelalah li-vrakhah):** The Hiphil of haphak = to overturn, turn upside down. The LORD's sovereign reversal of Balaam's intended curse is a key theological point. LXX: μετέστρεψεν ("changed/reversed"), similar.

**v.6 "their peace or their prosperity" (sholomam ve-tovotam):** Two nouns: shalom (peace, welfare) and tov (good, prosperity). The prohibition against seeking Ammon and Moab's shalom echoes and inverts the command to "seek the peace" (shalom) of Babylon in Jer 29:7. LXX: τὰ εἰρηνικὰ αὐτῶν καὶ τὰ συμφέροντα αὐτοῖς ("their peaceful things and what profits them"), matching the concept.

**v.10 "nocturnal emission" (mikreh laylaah):** Lit. "an occurrence of the night." The technical term for seminal emission during sleep, causing ritual impurity under Levitical law. "Nocturnal emission" is the established formal rendering. LXX: ρύσει νυκτερινῇ ("a night-time flow").

**v.13 "peg" (yated):** The same word used for tent pegs. In a military camp setting, a soldier's digging implement attached to his equipment. LXX: σκαλίς ("a shovel/hoe"), more specific about the implement's function.

**v.14 "indecent" (ervat davar):** Lit. "a matter of nakedness/exposure." The phrase ervat davar also appears in 24:1 in the divorce law, where it is the ground for divorce. LOGOS: "anything indecent" captures the range without over-specifying. LXX: ἀσχήμονα πρᾶγμα ("an indecent/shameful thing").

**v.17 "cult prostitute" (qedeshah / qadesh):** The feminine qedeshah and masculine qadesh literally mean "consecrated/holy woman/man." Whether these terms denote cultic sexual service is debated in modern scholarship. The project convention from Genesis (Gen 38) established "consecrated woman" for qedeshah. LOGOS applies the same convention here: "cult prostitute" is retained in the note only; the text uses "consecrated woman" and "consecrated man" but given the strong contextual pairing with prostitution fees in v.18, LOGOS uses "cult prostitute" to signal the sexual-cultic context that the law is addressing. LXX: πόρνη ("prostitute") and πόρνος ("male prostitute"), making the sexual function explicit.

**v.18 "wages of a dog" (etnan kelev):** "Dog" is a term of contempt that may refer to a male cult prostitute (qadesh) or simply a despised person. The pairing of "fee of a prostitute" and "wages of a dog" forms a merism for income from sexual vice. LXX: μίσθωμα πόρνης / ἄλλαγμα κυνός ("hire of a prostitute / exchange of a dog"), matching MT.

**v.19-20 "interest" (neshekh):** Neshekh = "bite" — interest that "bites" into the principal. The prohibition on lending at interest within the covenant community is covenantal solidarity; the foreigner (nokri) stands outside this solidarity network. LXX: τόκον ("offspring/interest"), a different metaphor.

**v.21 "will surely require it of you" (darsho yidreshennu):** Cognate infinitive reinforcing certainty: "seek/require he will require it." LXX: ἐκζητήσει ("will seek/require"), preserving the sense without the cognate construction.

**v.24-25 Gleaning-access rights:** The permission to eat from a neighbor's vineyard or grain field while passing through reflects ancient Near Eastern hospitality customs. The limitation (no bag, no sickle) prevents systematic harvesting while allowing incidental eating. This law governs the disciples' action in Mark 2:23/Matt 12:1 (plucking grain on the Sabbath).

— LOGOS
